svg.title: RepoLens AI — full system and GitHub workflow flowchart
svg.desc: Two flowcharts: top shows the system flow from user question to AI answer, bottom shows the GitHub development workflow for both team members.
svg.text: System flow — how a question becomes an answer

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about how the user asks a question in RepoLens AI')
sendPrompt('How does the Next.js frontend send requests to the backend?')
sendPrompt('What does the FastAPI backend do when it receives a request?')
sendPrompt('How does the query analyzer decide what kind of search to use?')
sendPrompt('How does the CAG cache work in RepoLens AI?')
g.text: Has this query been asked before?

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What happens when there is a cache hit?')
sendPrompt('How does Elasticsearch search the repository?')
sendPrompt('What does the re-ranker do in the search pipeline?')
sendPrompt('How does RAG work in RepoLens AI?')
g.text: Inject code context into LLM prompt

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Which LLM model does RepoLens AI use?')
g.text: With cited file names + line numbers

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does RepoLens AI display the final answer to the user?')
sendPrompt('How does RepoLens AI save results to the CAG cache?')

svg.text: GitHub development workflow — both team members

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do we create a GitHub issue in RepoLens AI?')
sendPrompt('What is the correct branch naming format for RepoLens AI?')
sendPrompt('What are best practices for writing commits in RepoLens AI?')
sendPrompt('How do we push a branch to GitHub?')
sendPrompt('What should a good pull request description include?')
sendPrompt('What are the checks before merging a PR in RepoLens AI?')
g.text: Move issue to Done on board
